label: NDYRA Live Release Closeout Packet
summary: Final operator packet for credentialed live execution. This does not claim deployment happened; it defines the evidence that must be captured before announcement.

items.label: Capture the live target identity
notes: Match the live target to /assets/build.json and the visible footer label before announcement.
Record the exact deployed URL and Netlify deploy identifier used for verification.

items.label: Record environment scope
notes: These values must match the environment that actually ran checkout, portal, webhook, and entitlement verification.

items.label: Prove schema truth is current
notes: The CP115 entitlement lifecycle migration must be present in the real database before runtime claims are trusted.

items.label: Capture deployed runtime truth
notes: Capture outputs/screenshots only after real values replace placeholders and blockers clear.

items.label: Verify member + business checkout
notes: Use the live verification matrix and confirm checkout/portal flows return to valid origins.

items.label: Verify token + entitlement flows
notes: Plan swaps must leave only the current sibling plan entitlement active.
Token top-up and purchased timer access must be reflected in wallet/purchases/library surfaces.

items.label: Run the final browser/device matrix
notes: Cover public pricing/join, member app shell, inbox/notifications unread sync, wallet/purchases, and admin status/execute.

items.label: Capture performance confidence
notes: Record the final performance sweep against the live target.

items.label: Record the release decision
notes: Do not mark release_status as announce_ready until all blocking items are complete with real evidence.

decision_rule: Do not announce NDYRA Core as live until all blocking items in this packet are filled with real values and the live verification matrix is complete.
If any blocker remains, keep the deployment in preview/staging status and record the blocker explicitly.
A finished packet requires deployment identity, schema/runtime truth, billing/entitlement verification, browser/device evidence, and performance evidence.
